# Design — useffect.sh landing page (v1)

**Date:** 2026-05-29
**Status:** Approved (design) — pending spec review
**Author:** Jérémy Magrin (with Claude Code)
**Design source:** `design/useffect.sh.html` (1,693 lines, generated by another AI)
**Replaces:** the bootstrap `// mounting...` teaser

## Goal

Replace the placeholder teaser at `useffect.sh` with the full landing page from
the provided HTML mockup, reimplemented as clean state-of-the-art Next.js code.

**Success condition:** on push to `main`, Vercel deploys a single-route landing
page faithfully reproducing the 8 sections of the design (Nav, Hero, Two Doors,
The us effect, Recent renders, Team, Process, Closer, Footer) — pixel-close to
the mockup, fully responsive, accessible, scoring high on Lighthouse, and
organized so that adding a 9th section or a `apps/mobile` consumer later is
mechanical, not architectural.

## Content treatment

**Decision:** Faithful 1:1 implementation. The AI-generated copy ships verbatim
— invented team personas, fabricated metrics, profanity in the hero
("WE UNFUCK THE FUCK CODE"), the "2 slots · Q3" status pill, the 6 case
studies, the "147 conference talks combined" footer figures, the easter-egg
console message. All of it. Treat the HTML mockup as the single source of
truth for copy.

No softening, no genericizing, no replacing with the real CODEOWNERS team in
this iteration. Iteration on copy is a future concern; this iteration is
about reproducing the design.

## Architecture

- **One Next.js page** (`/`) — replaces the current teaser.
- **React Server Components by default.** Client islands only where
  interactivity demands (4 islands total — see below).
- **Static at build time.** No data fetching, no ISR, no runtime data.
- **All code in `apps/web`.** `packages/` stays empty; the design system lives
  at `src/components/ui/` inside `apps/web` for now. When `apps/mobile` arrives,
  extract platform-neutral design tokens to `packages/tokens` first; UI
  primitives mostly won't cross-port (Tailwind ≠ RN StyleSheet).
- **Feature-sliced layout** under `apps/web/src/`. Each landing section is its
  own feature folder with co-located components, hooks, and data.

## File structure

```
apps/web/
├── src/
│   ├── app/                              Next.js App Router (routes only)
│   │   ├── layout.tsx                    metadata updated; analytics + fonts stay
│   │   ├── globals.css                   Tailwind v4 @theme + 3 small custom blocks
│   │   ├── page.tsx                      composition root, server
│   │   └── page.test.tsx                 smoke test
│   │
│   ├── components/                       generic, cross-feature
│   │   ├── section.tsx                   shared <section> wrapper (rule, eyebrow slot)
│   │   ├── console-egg.client.tsx        easter egg (single client island)
│   │   └── ui/                           design system primitives
│   │       ├── button.tsx                btn / btn.ghost / btn.lg / btn.xl / btn.invert
│   │       ├── container.tsx             1280px max + clamped gutter
│   │       ├── logo.tsx                  ~/u<s>ffect.sh
│   │       ├── eyebrow.tsx               "// foo" mono label + optional accent dot
│   │       ├── comment.tsx               "// thing" mono note (light/dark variants)
│   │       ├── status-pill.tsx           pulsing dot + label
│   │       ├── code-block.tsx            terminal chrome + syntax-token spans
│   │       └── cursor.tsx                pure-CSS blinking cursor
│   │
│   ├── features/                         one folder per landing section
│   │   ├── nav/
│   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   ├── nav.tsx               sticky header server shell
│   │   │   │   └── mobile-menu.client.tsx  <880px <dialog> drawer
│   │   │   └── hooks/
│   │   │       └── use-nav-scroll-state.client.ts  passive scroll listener
│   │   ├── hero/
│   │   │   └── components/
│   │   │       ├── hero.tsx              section root
│   │   │       ├── hero-headline.tsx     "WE UNFUCK THE FUCK CODE" + animated cursor
│   │   │       ├── hero-terminal.tsx     us.ts snippet via <CodeBlock>
│   │   │       └── hero-belt.tsx         4-cell stats strip
│   │   ├── services/
│   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   ├── services.tsx
│   │   │   │   └── door.tsx              one component, two instances (Rescue + Build)
│   │   │   └── data.ts                   typed door content
│   │   ├── effect/
│   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   ├── effect.tsx            server shell + full metrics table
│   │   │   │   └── effect-toggle.client.tsx  ARIA tablist + keyboard nav
│   │   │   └── data.ts                   6 metric rows
│   │   ├── missions/
│   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   ├── missions.tsx
│   │   │   │   └── mission-card.tsx
│   │   │   └── data.ts                   6 case studies
│   │   ├── team/
│   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   ├── team.tsx              dark section shell
│   │   │   │   ├── package-block.tsx     "your-app/package.json" pseudo-snippet
│   │   │   │   ├── member-card.tsx
│   │   │   │   └── portrait-svg.tsx      server-rendered SVG (gradient + grain + silhouette)
│   │   │   └── data.ts                   5 typed members + hues
│   │   ├── process/
│   │   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   │   ├── process.tsx
│   │   │   │   └── lifecycle-step.tsx    one of the 3 steps
│   │   │   └── data.ts                   3 steps
│   │   ├── closer/
│   │   │   └── components/
│   │   │       └── closer.tsx            final CTA (mailto)
│   │   └── footer/
│   │       ├── components/
│   │       │   ├── footer.tsx
│   │       │   ├── easter-line.tsx
│   │       │   └── footer-column.tsx
│   │       └── data.ts                   typed footer columns
│   │
│   └── lib/
│       └── cn.ts                         class-merging helper (only if 2+ files need it)
```

### Path config changes

- `apps/web/tsconfig.json` → `"paths": { "@/*": ["./src/*"] }` (was `["./*"]`).
- `apps/web/vitest.config.ts` → `alias '@'` resolves to `resolve(__dirname, 'src')`
  (was `'.'`).
- Existing files (`app/layout.tsx`, `app/page.tsx`, `app/globals.css`,
  `app/page.test.tsx`) **move** into `src/app/` — content-preserving moves, not
  rewrites.
- `next-env.d.ts` continues to land at `apps/web/` root (Next behavior with
  `src/app` layout); existing `apps/web/.gitignore` already covers it.

### Naming conventions

- `*.client.tsx` / `*.client.ts` = explicit `'use client'`. Everything else is
  server.
- Per-feature `data.ts` holds the TS interface(s) + the data array, exported as
  `as const satisfies Type` for full narrowing.
- Per-feature `hooks/` only when at least one hook lives there.
- A feature gets a nested `components/` folder when it has ≥2 components
  (all current features qualify).

### Imports

`src/app/page.tsx` reads like a table of contents:

```ts
import { Nav } from '@/features/nav/components/nav'
import { Hero } from '@/features/hero/components/hero'
import { Services } from '@/features/services/components/services'
import { Effect } from '@/features/effect/components/effect'
import { Missions } from '@/features/missions/components/missions'
import { Team } from '@/features/team/components/team'
import { Process } from '@/features/process/components/process'
import { Closer } from '@/features/closer/components/closer'
import { Footer } from '@/features/footer/components/footer'
import { ConsoleEgg } from '@/components/console-egg.client'
```

## CSS strategy — Tailwind v4 first

`apps/web/src/app/globals.css`:

```css
@import 'tailwindcss';

@theme inline {
  --color-bg: #FAFAF7;
  --color-bg-2: #F2F0E9;
  --color-ink: #0E0E0C;
  --color-ink-2: #1A1A17;
  --color-muted: #6B6B66;
  --color-muted-2: #9A9A93;
  --color-line: #E6E4DD;
  --color-line-strong: #D6D3C9;
  --color-accent: #00C853;
  --color-accent-ink: #003D19;
  --color-dark: #0B0B0A;
  --color-dark-2: #131311;
  --color-dark-line: #232320;
  --color-dark-muted: #8A8A82;
  --color-warn: #E5484D;

  --font-sans: var(--font-geist-sans);
  --font-mono: var(--font-geist-mono);

  --container-max-page: 1280px;

  --animate-blink: blink 1.05s steps(1) infinite;
  --animate-pulse-dot: pulse-dot 2.4s ease-in-out infinite;
}
```

This yields utilities like `bg-bg`, `text-ink`, `border-line`, `text-accent`,
`animate-blink`, `animate-pulse-dot` directly. ~95% of styling becomes Tailwind
utilities. Three small custom CSS blocks remain in `globals.css`:

1. The hero `<span class="strike">` strike-through animation on "FUCK CODE".
2. The terminal syntax-token classes (`.tok-c`, `.tok-k`, `.tok-fn`, `.tok-p`,
   `.tok-s`, `.tok-us`) — color-only.
3. The `@keyframes blink` and `@keyframes pulse-dot` declarations the `@theme`
   `--animate-*` tokens reference.

No CSS modules. No inline `style` props in JSX (rare exceptions allowed for the
single team-portrait gradient that needs dynamic HSL values per member —
inlined `style={{background: ...}}` is acceptable there).

## Client islands

Only 4 — everything else server-renders.

| Island | Why client | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `use-nav-scroll-state.client.ts` (hook) consumed by `nav.tsx` indirectly | scroll event | Listener on `window.scrollY`; toggles a `data-scrolled` attribute on the `<header>` once past 8px so the bottom border fades in. Passive listener, cleanup on unmount, respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (no motion-bound styling, just a border). The hook is the client island; the `<Nav>` shell stays server, wrapping a tiny `<NavScrollState>` client wrapper that applies the data attribute. |
| `mobile-menu.client.tsx` | open/close state | <880px hamburger. Uses semantic `<dialog>` with `showModal()`/`close()`. Locks body scroll while open. Closes on anchor click and on `Escape`. Focus trapped natively by `<dialog>`. |
| `effect-toggle.client.tsx` | tab state | `useState<'before' \| 'after'>('after')`. Renders both columns in the DOM; a `data-state` attribute on the wrapper drives `[data-state=...]` CSS selectors to show/hide. ARIA `role="tablist"`, `role="tab"`, `aria-selected`, `aria-controls`. Arrow-Left/Right keyboard navigation. |
| `console-egg.client.tsx` | side effect | One-shot `useEffect(() => console.log(...), [])`. Empty render. Mounted from `layout.tsx` so it fires on every page (only one page exists today). |

The blinking cursor and the pulsing status dot are **pure CSS animations** — no
JS, no client component required.

## Responsive

Design only ships one explicit breakpoint (`max-width: 880px` hides nav links).
The spec adds tasteful responsive defaults the mockup didn't spell out:

- **Hamburger drawer** at <880px (the `mobile-menu` island).
- Hero: terminal block stacks below copy at <960px; headline scales down at
  <640px.
- 12-col grid collapses progressively (lg/md/sm breakpoints from Tailwind).
- **us effect** table → stacked cards at <640px (the 4-column row is unusable
  on narrow screens).
- Missions grid: 2-col → 1-col at <768px.
- Team squad: 5-col → 3-col → 2-col → 1-col.
- Footer: 5-col → 2-col → 1-col.

## Accessibility

- Semantic landmarks: `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<section>`, `<article>`,
  `<footer>`.
- Skip-link `Skip to content` → `#main`, visible only on focus.
- Focus rings preserved via Tailwind `focus-visible:` utilities with
  accent-colored outlines.
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: disables `blink`, `pulse-dot`, button transforms,
  and any scroll-tied transitions. Implemented via `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`
  in `globals.css`.
- All interactive elements are real `<button>` or `<a>` — no clickable `<div>`s.
- The effect toggle is a proper ARIA tablist with keyboard navigation.
- The status pill's pulsing dot is decorative — `aria-hidden`.
- `<html lang="en">` preserved.
- Color contrast: design palette is AAA on the main canvas; verify the dark
  section's `--color-dark-muted` against `--color-dark` (AA target).

## Metadata

`src/app/layout.tsx` metadata is updated:

- `title`: `"useffect.sh — React Native Expert on Demand"` (the design's
  intended title).
- `description`: unchanged (`"A senior React Native collective. Series A/B
  startups only."`).
- OG + Twitter cards: unchanged.
- `metadataBase`: unchanged (`https://useffect.sh`).
- `/og.png` reference: stays — asset not built this iteration.

## Testing

Vitest + Testing Library. Additions only:

- **Page smoke test** (updated `page.test.tsx`):
  - Renders without throwing.
  - Asserts each section's defining text is present: hero headline, `Two doors.`,
    `The us effect.`, `Recent renders.`, `The dependencies.`,
    `Mount. Ship. Unmount.`, `// return ()`.
- **Effect toggle test** (`effect-toggle.test.tsx`):
  - Default state is `after`.
  - Click `before` flips `data-state` and `aria-selected`.
  - Arrow-Right key from `before` focuses and activates `after`.
- **Mobile menu test** (`mobile-menu.test.tsx`):
  - Clicking the hamburger opens the dialog.
  - `Escape` closes it.
  - Clicking an anchor link closes it.

No snapshot tests. No visual regression — those belong in a later iteration
with Chromatic or Playwright.

## Constraints

- **No commits or pushes by Claude.** The user reviews everything and runs all
  git commands. Plan tasks end in verification, never in `git commit`.
- **Bun only.** No `npm`, `pnpm`, or `yarn` invocations or references.
- **Pin nothing by hand.** Let `bun add` resolve. `bun.lock` is authoritative.
- **Strict TypeScript.** No `any`, no `@ts-ignore`, no `@ts-expect-error`.
- **Tailwind utilities first.** Custom CSS only for the three documented blocks
  + the team portrait gradient (inline `style={}` exception).
- **No new heavy deps.** No icon library, no framer-motion, no shadcn, no
  radix. The design needs none.
- The full local quality gate must pass: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck &&
  bun run test:ci && bun run build`.

## Out of scope (this iteration)

- `og.png` and favicon assets.
- Real contact form (`mailto:hello@useffect.sh` stays).
- Per-mission detail pages (anchors only, no `/missions/*` routes).
- Real `/team`, `/writing`, `/work` subpages — those anchor links resolve on
  this page only.
- Analytics dashboards, blog, CMS, real backend.
- E2E / visual regression tests.
- Internationalization (English only).
- A dark mode preference toggle (design has a dark section but not a dark
  theme).
- Replacing AI-generated copy with real copy.

## Manual steps (user, after merge)

- Review the diff and commit/push with a message of your choice.
- Vercel re-deploys automatically; verify the live page at `useffect.sh`
  matches the mockup.
- Optional: add `og.png` + favicon in a follow-up; they're already referenced
  in metadata.
